description: The catalog master for a purchasable material, spare, or product-design item. Carries a configurable structured code, an auto-populated description, stock and purchase units of measure, segment/sub-segment classification, a standard (preferred) supplier, HS code for import duty, and a status that mirrors the supplier lifecycle. Created via form, implicit auto-create from a non-UUID requisition line, or bulk import. ONBOARDED items feed requisition lines, PO lines, and GRN.
lifecycleEnum: ItemStatus (PENDING_ONBOARDING -> PENDING_APPROVAL -> ONBOARDED -> OFFBOARDED; plus SUSPENDED reversible)

fields.name: code
fields.label: Item Code
fields.validation: configurable pattern DEPARTMENT/SEGMENT_CODE/SUB_SEGMENT_CODE/RUNNING_NUMBER e.g. ME/SG/SSG/000001; dept ME|EL|AD|CP|SR|IT fallback DEP; seg/sub default SG/SSG; runningNumber 6-digit zero-padded (max+1, 0 if none), transactional/race-safe; uniqueness on (runningNumber, code)
fields.computedFormula: pattern-generated on create from dept/segment/subSegment codes + next runningNumber
fields.purpose: Stable human-readable item key that encodes department/segment/sub-segment classification and travels onto requisition lines, PO lines, GRN, and invoices; configurable pattern and race-safe running number keep codes unique and meaningful across catalogs.

fields.name: runningNumber
fields.label: Running Number
fields.validation: 6-digit zero-padded; max existing + 1 (0 if none); generated transactionally (race-safe); part of the uniqueness key (runningNumber, code)
fields.purpose: The monotonic per-catalog sequence that makes each item code unique within its department/segment; generated race-safe so concurrent creates and bulk imports never collide.

fields.name: shortDescription
fields.label: Short Description
fields.purpose: The buyer-entered item name that, combined with segment and sub-segment descriptions, builds the full auto-populated description; the core human label for the item.

fields.name: description
fields.label: Description
fields.validation: auto-populated = segmentDesc + subSegmentDesc + shortDescription; fallback to item code; for implicit auto-create = the free-typed requisition-line string
fields.purpose: The full descriptive text printed on requisitions, POs, and GRNs; auto-populated from classification + short description so descriptions are consistent, with a code fallback so the field is never empty.

fields.name: status
fields.label: Status
fields.validation: PENDING_ONBOARDING -> PENDING_APPROVAL -> ONBOARDED -> OFFBOARDED (terminal), plus SUSPENDED (reversible); edit on ONBOARDED/PENDING_APPROVAL reverts to PENDING_APPROVAL; approve requires code + description + stockUom + purchaseUom + >= 1 source priority
fields.purpose: The onboarding lifecycle position that gates general use: only ONBOARDED is orderable for general use (an auto-created PENDING_ONBOARDING item is usable on its own requisition immediately); edit reverts to re-approval and SUSPENDED blocks new ordering (axiom A10).

fields.name: stockUom
fields.label: Stock UoM
fields.validation: a configured UoM master; for implicit auto-create = the requisition line UoM
fields.purpose: The unit the item is stocked/counted in (the inventory base unit); the unit GRN quantities and stock-on-hand are expressed in, kept distinct from the purchase unit for items bought and stored in different units.

fields.name: purchaseUom
fields.label: Purchase UoM
fields.validation: a configured UoM master; for implicit auto-create = the requisition line UoM
fields.purpose: The unit the item is bought in; seeds the requisition-line and PO-line unit of measure so ordering and pricing use the purchasing unit even when it differs from the stock unit.

fields.name: segmentId
fields.label: Segment
fields.validation: a Segment master; defaults SG; its code forms part of the item code and its description forms part of the auto-populated description
fields.purpose: The top-level catalog classification of the item; drives the item code structure, the auto-populated description, and category-level spend reporting.

fields.name: subSegmentId
fields.label: Sub-Segment
fields.validation: a Sub-Segment master; defaults SSG; its code forms part of the item code and its description forms part of the auto-populated description
fields.purpose: The second-level catalog classification refining the segment; contributes to the item code and description and to finer-grained spend analytics.

fields.name: categoryId
fields.label: Category / Finished-Product Category
fields.validation: a Category master; used for spend reporting
fields.purpose: The reporting category (finished-product category) the item rolls up to for spend-cut analytics, distinct from the structural segment/sub-segment classification.

fields.name: hsCode
fields.label: HS Code
fields.purpose: The Harmonized System tariff code that classifies the item for customs so import duty, the landed-cost duty component, and customs documentation are computed correctly for import purchases.

fields.name: standardSupplierId
fields.label: Standard Supplier
fields.validation: nullable; need NOT be ONBOARDED (any existing supplier id or null accepted), unlike the PO supplier gate; on bulk import every standardSupplierCode must resolve or the whole import fails
fields.purpose: The preferred/default supplier for the item used to pre-fill sourcing and as a sourcing hint; deliberately not gated on ONBOARDED so the catalog can record intent before a supplier is fully qualified.

fields.name: regulatedItem
fields.label: Regulated Item
fields.purpose: Flags items subject to regulatory control (chemicals, pharma, dangerous goods) so the flow requires MSDS/permits/COA and the receiving QC gate and customs handling apply; an auditor checks regulated items for the extra documentation.

fields.name: isErpSynced
fields.label: ERP Synced
fields.validation: toggle only when status = ONBOARDED; reset to false on every edit
fields.purpose: Marks whether the ONBOARDED item master has been pushed across the ERP boundary; resetting on every edit (axiom A10) forces a re-sync after changes so the ERP never holds a stale unapproved item.

fields.name: reorderPoint
fields.label: Reorder Point
fields.validation: >= 0; in stockUom; when available (stockOnHand - allocated) falls at or below this value the item surfaces on the reorder worklist (axiom A21)
fields.purpose: The stock level at or below which a replenishment requisition should be raised; the trigger threshold the reorder worklist is filtered on so the Inventory Manager sees items needing action before they reach zero.

fields.name: safetyStock
fields.label: Safety Stock
fields.validation: >= 0; in stockUom; the buffer held against demand variability and lead-time uncertainty; typically <= reorderPoint
fields.purpose: The minimum buffer quantity held to protect against demand variability and supplier lead-time uncertainty; informs the suggested replenishment quantity and the urgency of a reorder trigger.

fields.name: minStock
fields.label: Minimum Stock
fields.validation: >= 0; in stockUom; operational floor below which the item is considered critically low; typically >= safetyStock
fields.purpose: The operational minimum quantity; dropping below this is treated as a critical shortage warranting expedited requisition or escalation, distinct from the reorder trigger.

fields.name: maxStock
fields.label: Maximum Stock
fields.validation: > 0; in stockUom; the suggested replenishment ceiling; suggestedQty = maxStock - available (axiom A21); must be > reorderPoint when both set
fields.purpose: The maximum target stock quantity used to compute the suggested replenishment order quantity (maxStock minus available) so orders neither under-fill nor over-fill the warehouse slot.

invariants: A10 edit on ONBOARDED/PENDING_APPROVAL reverts to PENDING_APPROVAL; ERP-sync flag resets on update; SUSPENDED blocks new ordering (reversible)
A13 audit (category ADMIN) + SSE on every change
A21 available (stockOnHand - allocated) at or below reorderPoint surfaces the item on the reorder worklist; suggestedQty = maxStock - available
code uniqueness on (runningNumber, code); runningNumber 6-digit zero-padded generated transactionally
